0x8024402F.description: External CAB processing completed with errors. Web-filtering software can alter or block update responses.

recommendations.action: Confirm that web filtering or SSL inspection is not modifying Windows Update, WSUS, SUP, or content-download traffic.
Add the affected device or required update endpoints to the approved exception list, according to the organization network policy.
Retry the scan and review WindowsUpdate.log and WUAHandler.log for the same code.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80242006.description: The update handler could not complete because the update metadata is invalid.

recommendations.action: Redownload the update content and verify that the deployment package content is healthy on the Distribution Point.
Review WUAHandler.log, WindowsUpdate.log, CAS.log, and ContentTransferManager.log before resetting any local cache.
If corruption is confirmed, rename the Windows Update datastore/download folders and Catroot2 during a controlled maintenance window, then rescan.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070BC9.description: The requested operation failed because a restart is required to complete or roll back servicing changes.

recommendations.action: Restart the device and allow pending servicing operations to finish before triggering another update evaluation.
Confirm that Windows Installer and TrustedInstaller startup behavior is not being forced incorrectly by Group Policy.
If the condition persists after restart, review CBS.log and pending servicing state before considering offline recovery actions.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80200053.description: BITS validation failed, potentially because a firewall or content filter altered the downloaded response.

recommendations.action: Validate that firewall, proxy, antivirus, or SSL inspection is not changing update content in transit.
Check BITS jobs and review DataTransferService.log and ContentTransferManager.log for the failed file or URL.
Retry the content download after confirming connectivity to the assigned Distribution Point or update source.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80072EFD.description: The update operation timed out or could not establish a connection to the update source.

recommendations.action: Validate connectivity from the device to its configured WSUS/SUP and assigned Distribution Point.
Check proxy and firewall rules for blocked update or content URLs.
Review WindowsUpdate.log, WUAHandler.log, LocationServices.log, and network traces for the failed endpoint.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80072EFE.description: The connection to the server was closed abnormally while transferring update content.

recommendations.action: Inspect active BITS jobs and identify the HTTP status and file associated with the failure.
Validate proxy and network access to the configured WSUS/SUP or Distribution Point using the organization proxy settings.
Review DataTransferService.log, ContentTransferManager.log, and WindowsUpdate.log for repeated connection resets.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80D02002.description: The download operation made no progress within the defined period.

recommendations.action: Confirm that the required update content is distributed and available on the device assigned Distribution Point.
Validate Delivery Optimization, BITS, proxy, firewall, and network connectivity to the content source.
Review CAS.log, LocationServices.log, ContentTransferManager.log, DataTransferService.log, and Delivery Optimization event logs.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8007000D.description: Invalid or corrupted data was downloaded.

recommendations.action: Redownload the update and confirm that the deployment package content is healthy on the Distribution Point.
Review CAS.log, ContentTransferManager.log, DataTransferService.log, and CBS.log for corruption or hash-validation evidence.
If repeated across multiple devices, validate or redistribute the SCCM deployment package content.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8024A10A.description: The Windows Update service is shutting down, commonly after inactivity or a lost connection.

recommendations.action: Keep the device online and active long enough for the update scan and installation to complete.
Confirm that Windows Update, BITS, and CCMExec services remain running during evaluation.
Review WUAHandler.log and WindowsUpdate.log for service shutdown or connection-loss events.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80240020.description: The operation did not complete because no interactive user was signed in.

recommendations.action: Confirm whether the update or installer genuinely requires an interactive user session.
Review deployment user-experience settings and ensure the update can run in the intended system context.
Sign in during a controlled test and review UpdatesDeployment.log and WindowsUpdate.log to confirm the requirement.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80242014.description: The post-restart update operation is still pending.

recommendations.action: Restart the device and allow Windows servicing to finish before forcing another evaluation.
Confirm the device is not repeatedly interrupted or shut down during post-reboot processing.
Review RebootCoordinator.log, UpdatesDeployment.log, and CBS.log after the restart.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80246017.description: The local user was not authorized to download or install the update.

recommendations.action: Confirm that the deployment runs in the required system or administrative context.
Validate local policy and permissions affecting Windows Update and content-download locations.
Review WUAHandler.log and WindowsUpdate.log for the exact authorization failure.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8024000B.description: The operation was canceled by the user or a service.

recommendations.action: Identify whether the deployment, client service, restart, or user action canceled the operation.
Review UpdatesDeployment.log, WUAHandler.log, and WindowsUpdate.log around the last status time.
Retry the evaluation only after eliminating the cancellation trigger.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8024000E.description: Windows Update Agent found invalid XML data in update metadata.

recommendations.action: Confirm that the Windows Update Agent and current servicing stack are up to date.
Review WindowsUpdate.log and WUAHandler.log to identify the update or driver with invalid metadata.
Validate WSUS/SUP synchronization health and decline or correct malformed third-party update metadata if applicable.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8024D009.description: A Windows Update Agent self-update was skipped by directive, potentially because WSUS did not provide the required self-update.

recommendations.action: Validate WSUS/SUP configuration and client access to the WSUS self-update path.
Confirm that the client is using the intended WSUS/SUP policy and can reach the configured server.
Review WindowsUpdate.log, WUAHandler.log, and WSUS/IIS logs for self-update failures.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80244007.description: The Windows Update SOAP client failed, often because update cookies could not be renewed.

recommendations.action: Validate communication with the configured WSUS/SUP and review IIS and WSUS health.
Check WindowsUpdate.log and WUAHandler.log for cookie, SOAP, or server-response details.
Confirm that proxy, SSL inspection, and authentication are not interfering with WSUS communication.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070422.description: The Windows Update service is disabled or not running.

recommendations.action: Check that the Windows Update service is enabled and running with the organization approved startup configuration.
Identify any Group Policy, security baseline, or remediation that repeatedly disables the service.
Restart the service, trigger a software update scan, and review WUAHandler.log and WindowsUpdate.log.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F0821.description: The CBS servicing transaction exceeded its watchdog timeout.

recommendations.action: Allow additional time and ensure the device has sufficient CPU, memory, and disk resources for servicing.
Review CBS.log and DISM.log for the component or operation that stopped progressing.
For virtual machines, validate resource contention before retrying the update.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F0825.description: A package cannot be uninstalled, commonly because the component store is partially installed or corrupted.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth from an elevated prompt and review DISM.log.
Run SFC /Scannow after DISM completes, then restart the device.
If repair fails, identify the missing payload in CBS.log and use a matching repair source.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F0920.description: Windows servicing detected that an operation stopped responding.

recommendations.action: Ensure sufficient CPU, memory, disk space, and uninterrupted runtime for the servicing operation.
Review CBS.log and DISM.log to identify the package or phase that stopped responding.
For virtual machines, resolve resource contention before retrying.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F081F.description: The source files required for component-store repair or update installation could not be found.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and review DISM.log for the exact missing source.
If online repair is restricted, provide a repair source that exactly matches the installed Windows version, edition, language, and patch level.
Run SFC /Scannow after DISM succeeds, restart the device, and retry the update.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F0831.description: The Windows component store is corrupted or has a partially installed component.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and review DISM.log and CBS.log.
Run SFC /Scannow after DISM completes, then restart the device.
If repair fails, identify the missing package dependency and use a matching repair source.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070005.description: The servicing stack was denied access to a required file, folder, or registry key.

recommendations.action: Review the latest CBS.log around the failure timestamp and identify the exact object returning access denied.
Validate whether antivirus, endpoint protection, hardening, or modified ACLs are blocking the servicing stack.
Correct only the affected permission according to the expected Windows default, then retry the update.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070570.description: A required file or directory is corrupted and unreadable.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and review DISM.log and CBS.log.
Run SFC /Scannow and check disk health if file corruption is suspected.
Restart the device after repair and retry the update.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070003.description: The servicing stack could not access a required path or executable.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log around the failure timestamp to identify the missing path.
Validate that security software, cleanup tasks, or a previous failed update did not remove the required file or directory.
Repair the component store or restore the exact missing component before retrying.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070020.description: A required file is in use, commonly because a non-Microsoft filter driver or security product is holding it.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log to identify the file involved in the sharing violation.
Use a controlled clean boot or Process Monitor capture to identify the process or filter driver holding the file.
Retry the update only after the conflicting process is safely stopped or remediated.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80073701.description: A referenced side-by-side assembly could not be found.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and review DISM.log and CBS.log.
Run SFC /Scannow, restart the device, and retry the update.
If the assembly remains missing, use a matching repair source or repair the identified package dependency.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8007371B.description: A component-store transaction is incomplete because required members are missing.

recommendations.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and review DISM.log and CBS.log.
Run SFC /Scannow, restart the device, and retry the update.
If repair fails, identify the incomplete package transaction in CBS.log and use a matching repair source.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80072F8F.description: Windows Update could not decode the received content, commonly because TLS 1.2 is not configured correctly.

recommendations.action: Validate TLS 1.2 configuration for WinHTTP and the Windows Update Agent.
Confirm certificate trust, system date/time, proxy inspection, and secure connectivity to WSUS/SUP.
Review WindowsUpdate.log, Schannel events, and WUAHandler.log for TLS or decoding failures.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80072EE2.description: Windows Update Agent timed out while connecting to Windows Update, Configuration Manager, WSUS, or another update source.

recommendations.action: Validate connectivity to the configured WSUS/SUP and, for content, the assigned Distribution Point.
Check proxy, firewall, DNS, certificate, and network routing for the update-source FQDN.
Review WindowsUpdate.log, WUAHandler.log, LocationServices.log, and IIS/WSUS logs.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80240022.description: The operation failed for all updates; antivirus or another filter may be blocking required update folders.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log and WindowsUpdate.log to identify the first concrete failure rather than treating this aggregate code as the root cause.
Validate whether antivirus or endpoint protection is blocking SoftwareDistribution, Catroot2, WinSxS, or servicing operations.
Retry only after the underlying file, folder, or servicing failure is identified and corrected.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8024401B.description: The update source returned HTTP 407 because proxy authentication is required.

recommendations.action: Validate WinHTTP proxy configuration used by Windows Update and ensure the update-source FQDN is reachable.
Confirm with the network team that WSUS/SUP traffic does not require interactive user authentication.
Review WindowsUpdate.log and WUAHandler.log for the proxy endpoint and authentication response.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80244022.description: The update source returned HTTP 503 and is temporarily unavailable or overloaded.

recommendations.action: Validate WSUS/SUP and IIS service health, application pools, disk space, and database connectivity.
Confirm that proxy or load-balancer infrastructure is not returning the 503 response.
Review WUAHandler.log, WindowsUpdate.log, IIS logs, and WSUS health before retrying the scan.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x80070490.description: Required driver architecture information is missing during update installation.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log and the DriverOperations registry data to identify the driver entry with missing architecture metadata.
Validate the affected driver package and replace or repair it using an approved vendor source.
Retry the update after correcting the specific driver metadata issue.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800F0922.description: A servicing installer failed; the exact cause must be confirmed in CBS.log.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log for license, product-key token, permission, or installer failure details at the update timestamp.
Validate free space and permissions on servicing-related system locations before changing any ACL.
Apply only the remediation supported by the evidence found in CBS.log, then restart and retry.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x800706BE.description: The update package or servicing state may be corrupted, causing an RPC or installation failure.

recommendations.action: Review CBS.log and DISM.log to identify the corrupted package and its current state.
Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth and SFC /Scannow before making manual registry changes.
Escalate manual package-state repair only after backup and validation of the exact corrupted package.

source.title: Windows Update common errors and mitigation

0x8007066A.description: The upgrade cannot be installed because the product may be missing or the update targets another version.

recommendations.action: Confirm that the target product and exact version required by the update are installed.
Validate the update applicability, architecture, language, and product code against the device inventory.
Review MSI logging and UpdatesDeployment.log to identify the product or patch applicability mismatch.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge

0x87D00319.description: Configuration Manager could not detect the application after installation or the detection rule returned an unexpected result.

recommendations.action: Validate the application detection method against the actual post-installation state on the device.
Review AppEnforce.log and AppDiscovery.log for the executed command, exit code, and detection result.
Correct the detection rule or installation behavior, then rerun application evaluation.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge

0x800F081E.description: The update package is not applicable to this computer.

recommendations.action: Confirm the operating system version, build, architecture, language, and prerequisite servicing stack.
Validate that the deployment contains the correct update for this device and supersedence state.
Review CBS.log and WUAHandler.log for the specific applicability reason.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge

0x80244017.description: The update service denied access, commonly corresponding to HTTP 403.

recommendations.action: Validate client access to the configured WSUS/SUP and confirm IIS authentication and authorization settings.
Check proxy, SSL inspection, certificates, and network controls that could return HTTP 403.
Review WindowsUpdate.log, WUAHandler.log, and IIS logs for the rejected request.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge

0x80244010.description: Windows Update exceeded the maximum number of server round trips while searching for updates.

recommendations.action: Retry the scan after allowing WSUS/SUP synchronization and client metadata processing to complete.
Validate WSUS health, product/classification scope, supersedence cleanup, and database performance.
Review WUAHandler.log and WindowsUpdate.log for repeated scan rounds or metadata issues.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge

0x80240437.description: The update search could not complete because the service returned an unexpected response.

recommendations.action: Review WindowsUpdate.log and WUAHandler.log for the underlying service or HTTP response.
Validate WSUS/SUP health, proxy behavior, TLS, and client policy configuration.
Retry the scan only after confirming connectivity and service health.

source.title: SCCM Patch Dashboard curated knowledge
